ddls:
CREATE TABLE "projections" (
  "projection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each projection */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the projection */,
  "fit" REAL /* Fit value of the projection model */,
  "lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the projection interval */,
  "higher" REAL /* Higher bound of the projection interval */,
  "tau" REAL /* Tau value of the projection model */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region of the projection */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parameter of the projection */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the model of the projection */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the projection was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the projection was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("projection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projections_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projections_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES projection_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projections_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the region in square kilometers */,
  "gdp_per_capita" REAL /* GDP per capita of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter (e.g., age, population growth rate) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the parameter (e.g., years, percentage) */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the parameter (e.g., integer, float) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projection_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model (e.g., linear, non-linear) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the model (e.g., statistical, machine learning) */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projection_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the model of the version */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the version was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the version was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projection_versions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES projection_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "projection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the projection being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the projection */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the projection was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_projection_id FOREIGN KEY ("projection_id") REFERENCES projections ("projection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projection_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "projection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the projection requested */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the request */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date when the request was made */,
  "request_status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or additional information about the request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projection_requests_projection_id FOREIGN KEY ("projection_id") REFERENCES projections ("projection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projection_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projection_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "projection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the projection used to generate the report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, Excel) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projection_reports_projection_id FOREIGN KEY ("projection_id") REFERENCES projections ("projection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projection_parameters_values" (
  "parameter_value_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter value */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parameter */,
  "projection_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the projection */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the parameter */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the parameter value (e.g., census, survey) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_value_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projection_parameters_values_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projection_parameters_values_projection_id FOREIGN KEY ("projection_id") REFERENCES projections ("projection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projection_model_parameters" (
  "model_parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model parameter */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the model */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parameter */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the parameter in the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projection_model_parameters_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES projection_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projection_model_parameters_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projection_model_versions" (
  "model_version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model version */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the model */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projection_version_parameters" (
  "version_parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version parameter */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the version */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parameter */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the parameter in the version */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the version parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission (e.g., view_projections, edit_projections) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
